===''Pokémon the Series''=== [[File:Aaron anime.png|thumb|220px|Aaron in {{aniseries|DP}}]] [[File:Young Aaron.png|thumb|left|220px|Aaron when he was younger]] ====''Pokémon the Series: Diamond and Pearl''==== Aaron was briefly mentioned by fellow [[Elite Four]] member [[Lucian]] in ''[[DP035|An Elite Meet and Greet!]]''. He then debuted in ''[[DP099|A Trainer and Child Reunion!]]'', where he was shown to be preparing for his championship {{pkmn|battle}} against {{an|Cynthia}}. He was conducting a public {{pkmn|training}} session at a small town between [[Hearthome City]] and [[Solaceon Town]], which had been advertised in an issue of the {{DL|Magazines in the Pokémon world|Poké Chic}} magazine that featured him on the cover. {{Ash}} and {{ashfr}} decided to go watch Aaron train and joined the long line of fans that were there to meet him, with {{an|Dawn}} getting her copy of Poké Chic signed by him. When Aaron found out that the group knew Cynthia personally, he invited them to his training center. There, he revealed that a {{p|Wurmple}} was his [[List of characters' first Pokémon|first Pokémon]] and close friend when he was young, but due to his anger at it for losing a match, he abandoned it. He regretted it later, and from then on, he tried his best to train hard and to understand {{type|Bug}} {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} so that the same incident would not happen again. Aaron eventually encountered Wurmple again as a {{TP|Aaron|Beautifly}} by the end of the episode, following a battle against {{TRT}}. Aaron reappeared in [[DP100|the next episode]], where he was seen on [[television]] having his battle with Cynthia at the {{DL|Pokémon League|Champion League}}, but he lost. ====[[Pokémon movie]]s==== Aaron made a cameo appearance in the ending credits of ''[[M13|Zoroark: Master of Illusions]]''. ====Character==== Aaron likes to be surrounded by nature and {{type|Bug}} Pokémon. He tends to train in the forests, which allow him to rest up for upcoming challenges. He also enjoys the attention given by the status of being an [[Elite Four]] member. In addition, Aaron places [[friendship]] in high value, particularly with his Pokémon. ====Pokémon==== {{MissingInfo|2|enva|java}} This listing is of Aaron's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}: {{trainerPoké |main=yes |trainer=Aaron |pkmn=Beautifly |stage1=Wurmple |stage2=Silcoon |type1=Bug |type2=Flying |img=Aaron Beautifly.png |epnum=DP099 |epname=A Trainer and Child Reunion! |vajp=Kiyotaka Furushima |vaen=Michele Knotz |desc=Aaron first encountered his {{p|Beautifly}} as a {{p|Wurmple}} when it helped him down from a high tree. They became very close friends, but finally Aaron became irritated that it always lost in {{pkmn|battle}} and abandoned it in a park. When he later returned to find Wurmple, it was gone. The same Wurmple returned in ''[[DP099|A Trainer and Child Reunion!]]'', where it was revealed to have since [[Evolution|evolved]] into a Beautifly. They reunited beside the very same tree where they had first met. It informed Aaron that it decided to start training by itself to become stronger. Beautifly then helped Aaron, {{Ash}}, and {{ashfr}} to battle {{TRT}} after they tried to steal Aaron's Pokémon. After this, Aaron took Beautifly back into his [[party|team]]. Beautifly reappeared in [[DP100|the next episode]], where Aaron used it as his last Pokémon in his battle against Cynthia. It went up against Cynthia's {{p|Gastrodon}}, but it was [[Fainting|knocked out]] by Gastrodon's powerful {{m|Stone Edge}}.}} {{trainerPoké |trainer=Aaron |pkmn=Skorupi |type1=Poison |type2=Bug |img=Aaron Skorupi.png |epnum=DP099 |epname=A Trainer and Child Reunion! |vajp=Katsuyuki Konishi |desc=Aaron was seen training with a {{p|Skorupi}} in the lead-up to his match against {{an|Cynthia}}. It usually remains [[Walking Pokémon|outside of its Poké Ball]], and was seen on Aaron's shoulder. None of Skorupi's moves are known.}} {{trainerPoké |trainer=Aaron |pkmn=Drapion |type1=Poison |type2=Dark |img=Aaron Drapion.png |epnum=DP099 |epname=A Trainer and Child Reunion! |desc={{p|Drapion}} was first seen during Aaron's training exhibition in the lead-up to his {{pkmn|battle}} with {{an|Cynthia}}. When Drapion was stolen by {{TRT}} along with Aaron's other Pokémon, it attempted to attack [[Jessie's Seviper]], but it got badly hurt by Seviper's {{m|Poison Tail}}. Later, it was able to defeat Seviper with a Hyper Beam. During Aaron's battle against Cynthia in [[DP100|the following episode]], Drapion was sent out to face [[Cynthia's Garchomp]], but was defeated off-screen. Drapion's known moves are {{m|Pin Missile}} and {{m|Hyper Beam}}.}} {{trainerPoké | trainer = Aaron | pkmn = Vespiquen | type1 = Bug | type2 = Flying | gender = female | img = Aaron Vespiquen.png | epnum = DP099 | epname = A Trainer and Child Reunion! | vajp = Chinami Nishimura | desc = Aaron used {{p|Vespiquen}} along with his Drapion during his power demonstration. When she was captured by {{TRT}}, she was critically hurt by [[James's Carnivine]]'s {{m|Bullet Seed}}, but she was later able to defeat it with a Slash. Vespiquen's known moves are {{m|Slash}} and {{m|Gust}}. }} ====Voice actors==== {{vatable|color={{bug color}}|bordercolor={{bug color light}} |ja=小野賢章 ''[[Kenshō Ono]]''
